name: Nominated contacts (optional)
pageTitle: Nominated Contacts - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Contacts
heading: Nominated contacts (optional)
bodyCopy: Nominate up to 5 contacts. They will be notified if your consignment is chosen for inspection.
backLink: gap - no back link observed in main content region of the captured snapshot

structure.text: Nominate up to 5 contacts. They will be notified if your consignment is chosen for inspection.
structure.govukComponent: Body text (govuk-body), wrapped in a div id=nominated-contact-page-hint that both the fieldset and the table reference via aria-describedby (the id is on the wrapping div, not on the paragraph itself)

structure.text: Column headers: Name | Email address | Mobile number | (visually-hidden) Remove contact details
structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table) laying out the contact rows
Non-standard link-button (submit, name=remove-nominated-contact, value=0) in the row-actions cell
Link (govuk-link) href .../overview, wrapped in govuk-body paragraph

govukComponents.usage: Wraps the whole contacts entry area; legend holds the caption + H1, aria-describedby points at the div id=nominated-contact-page-hint that wraps the hint paragraph (the table also references the same id).
The 'Nominate up to 5 contacts...' hint paragraph and the paragraph wrapping the 'Cancel and return to hub' link.
Lays out the contact rows in columns Name / Email address / Mobile number / (remove). Inputs are associated to column headers via aria-labelledby rather than <label>. id=nominated-contacts-table.

Name (width-20, autocomplete=name), Email address (default width, type=email, spellcheck=false, autocomplete=email), Mobile number (width-20, type=tel, autocomplete=tel). Each wrapped in a govuk-form-group.
Wraps each of the three inputs inside its table cell.
'Save and return to hub' (name=save-and-return-button) and 'Save and continue' (id=button-save-and-continue) - both type=submit with data-prevent-double-click.
'Cancel and return to hub' link back to the notification overview/hub.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders submit buttons styled to look like inline text links. 'Add another person' posts add-nominated-contact to append a contact row; 'Remove' posts remove-nominated-contact with the row index to delete a row. Both are server round-trips (type=submit), not client-side row manipulation.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: link-button is a bespoke IPAFFS class, not a Design System component. GOV.UK has no 'button that looks like a link' pattern in this form; the govuk way is either a secondary button (govuk-button--secondary) or the 'Add another' add-to-a-list pattern. The add/remove is done via full page POSTs, which is a design choice the new app should decide on deliberately.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK 'Add another' add-to-a-list pattern, with govuk-button--secondary for Add and a link/secondary button for Remove; or a purpose-built repeatable-contact component.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A dangling 'govuk' class with no BEM block/element - it applies no defined Design System styling on its own.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Almost certainly a template typo (an unfinished 'govuk-...' class). Cosmetic dead class; flagged so the rebuild does not copy it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Remove; use the proper component class.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 90 (class inventory shows a lone 'govuk' token)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom responsive-table wrapper (likely enabling horizontal scroll / reflow of the multi-column contact grid on narrow viewports).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System class. GOV.UK does not ship a responsive-table wrapper; IPAFFS added its own. The multi-input-per-row table layout with header-associated inputs (aria-labelledby to <th>) is itself a non-standard form pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Prefer the 'Add another' pattern with each contact as a stacked set of properly-labelled fields (label per input) rather than a data table of inputs; that removes the need for a responsive-table hack.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: form inputs labelled via aria-labelledby to column <th> headers, no <label> elements in main
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Each input (name/email/telephone) is associated to its column header (Name/Email address/Mobile number) via aria-labelledby rather than a visible govuk-label.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Works for accessibility but is a table-of-inputs pattern, not the standard one-label-per-field GOV.UK form. If the rebuild keeps a repeatable list, each field should have its own visible label per instance.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk-label per input within an add-another list item.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 90 (main.html; 'Array.from(main label)' returned empty)

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.conditionalOn: Per-contact row; the page as a whole is optional for CHED-D (whole page can be skipped via Save and continue). Rows added/removed via Add another person / Remove. Legacy: name is conditionally required WITHIN a row being saved — if that row supplies an email or a telephone, name becomes required (Joi 'then: Joi.object({ name: Joi.required() })'). ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/nominated_contact.js:28-58.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 90 - input#name type=text, govuk-input--width-20, autocomplete=name, aria-labelledby=name-header ('Name'), required=false. Corroborated optional: the CHED-D workflow drives this page by clicking Save and continue with the name field left blank (ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:339-340, :659-660), so an empty name row is accepted. legacy: max length 32 chars — nominated_contact.js:28 (Joi.string().max(32)); validation label 'Name' — validation/labels/en.js:67. Page-level (list) not mandatory for CHED-D: PartOne.nominatedContacts carries @NotEmpty ONLY for NotificationTransporterContactDetailsEuFieldValidation (high-risk EU CHED-A transporter mode), not the CED group — ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/.../representation/PartOne.java:641-644.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.conditionalOn: Per-contact row; page optional for CHED-D. Legacy: when a contact row is being saved, at least one of email OR telephone is required alongside name (Joi '.or(EMAIL, TELEPHONE)' with OBJECT_MISSING message). nominated_contact.js:54-56.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 90 - input#email type=email, govuk-input, autocomplete=email, spellcheck=false, aria-labelledby=email-header ('Email address'), required=false. Corroborated optional: workflow skips the page with email blank (ched-d-workflows.ts:339-340, :659-660). legacy: max length 255 chars and RFC email-format check (Joi.string().max(255).email({ ignoreLength: true })) — nominated_contact.js:33-36; validation label 'Email address' — validation/labels/en.js:46.

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Mobile number
fields.conditionalOn: Per-contact row; page optional for CHED-D. Legacy: counts as one of the email-OR-telephone pair required when a row is saved (see email). nominated_contact.js:54-56.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 90 - input#telephone type=tel, govuk-input--width-20, autocomplete=tel, aria-labelledby=phone-header ('Mobile number'), required=false. NB: HTML name/id is 'telephone' but the visible column header/label is 'Mobile number'; the VALIDATION label used in error copy is 'Telephone number' (validation/labels/en.js:98) — a third naming variant. Corroborated optional: workflow skips the page with mobile number blank (ched-d-workflows.ts:339-340, :659-660). legacy: max length 30 chars and a TELEPHONE_REGEX pattern check (Joi.string().max(30).pattern(TELEPHONE_REGEX)) — nominated_contact.js:37-40; regex source ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/regexes.js (TELEPHONE_REGEX).

fields.name: contactsCount
fields.label: (cap) number of nominated contacts
fields.control: n/a - derived cap, not an input
fields.conditionalOn: Body copy states 'Nominate up to 5 contacts'. Legacy enforces the 5-contact cap server-side via the remove-index bound (Joi.number().min(0).max(4) on remove-nominated-contact, i.e. valid indices 0-4 = 5 rows) and the template's showNominatedContactInput flag which hides the entry row + 'Add another person' once the list is full.
fields.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/nominated_contact.js:41 (REMOVE_NOMINATED_CONTACT: Joi.number().min(0).max(4)); template views/importer/nominatedContact.html:90-138 ({{#if showNominatedContactInput}} gates both the input row and the Add-another-person button). Closes the trace's open question about whether the 'up to 5' cap is enforced — it is.

validationMessages.trigger: A contact row is being saved (email or telephone supplied, or the row is otherwise submitted) but the name is blank. Conditional 'then: Joi.object({ name: Joi.required() })'.
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:28-31,54 - requiredMessage(labels.NAME, labels.A). requiredMessage = `Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}` (validation/messages/en.js:31-33); labels.NAME='Name' (validation/labels/en.js:67), labels.A='a' (validation/labels/en.js:2) -> 'Enter a name'. Legacy mandatoriness: conditional per-row rule the rebuild may revisit.

validationMessages.message: Name must be 32 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Name entered is longer than 32 characters.
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:28-29 - maxLengthMessage(labels.NAME, 32). maxLengthMessage = `${label} must be ${maxLength} characters or fewer` (validation/messages/en.js:3-5); labels.NAME='Name' -> 'Name must be 32 characters or fewer'.

validationMessages.message: Enter an email address or mobile number
validationMessages.trigger: A contact row is being saved with a name but neither an email address nor a telephone number. Conditional '.or(EMAIL, TELEPHONE)' with OBJECT_MISSING.
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:54-56 - EMAIL_OR_TELEPHONE constant = 'Enter an email address or mobile number' (validation/messages/en.js:73). Legacy conditional rule.

validationMessages.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com
validationMessages.trigger: Email address is not a valid email format.
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:33-35 - Joi.string().email({ ignoreLength: true }) with STRING_EMAIL -> EMAIL_CORRECT_FORMAT constant (validation/messages/en.js:72).

validationMessages.message: Email address must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Email address entered is longer than 255 characters.
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:33-34 - maxLengthMessage(labels.EMAIL_ADDRESS, 255). labels.EMAIL_ADDRESS='Email address' (validation/labels/en.js:46) -> 'Email address must be 255 characters or fewer'.

validationMessages.message: Enter a mobile number in the correct format, like 07700 900 982 or +44 7700 900 982
validationMessages.trigger: Mobile number does not match the accepted telephone-number pattern (TELEPHONE_REGEX).
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:37-39 - Joi.string().pattern(TELEPHONE_REGEX) with STRING_PATTERN -> TELEPHONE_NUMBER_CORRECT_FORMAT constant (validation/messages/en.js:110).

validationMessages.message: Telephone number must be 30 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Mobile number entered is longer than 30 characters.
validationMessages.evidence: nominated_contact.js:37-38 - maxLengthMessage(labels.TELEPHONE_NUMBER, 30). labels.TELEPHONE_NUMBER='Telephone number' (validation/labels/en.js:98) -> 'Telephone number must be 30 characters or fewer'. NB copy says 'Telephone number' though the field's on-screen column header is 'Mobile number' - naming inconsistency for the rebuild to resolve.

validationMessages.message: gap - no validation errors were rendered in this trace (0 errors on this trace, and the page was skipped empty via Save and continue). The field rules are now supplied by legacy (see the legacy-tagged messages above); none was observed rendering.
validationMessages.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 90 - govuk-error-summary absent; no error snapshot. QA repo corroborates the rendering gap: the page object (ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/NominatedContactsPage.ts:6-8) exposes ONLY btnSaveAndContinue, and no CHED-D spec drives or asserts any nominated-contacts field or error. The rules themselves are no longer a gap - they are now sourced from the legacy frontend validation (nominated_contact.js) - but they remain unverified by any rendered trace or test.

openQuestions: The page was submitted empty (no contacts entered) via Save and continue, so no validation was exercised. What are the field rules - email format, mobile-number format, is a partial row (name only) allowed, and is the stated 'up to 5 contacts' cap enforced server-side? Needs an error-state trace or the page object/template to confirm.

Add another person and Remove are server round-trip POSTs (name=add-nominated-contact / remove-nominated-contact with row index). Confirm whether the new app should keep server-side add/remove or move to a client add-to-a-list.
The captured snapshot's main region had no back link; IPAFFS navigation may place the back link outside main. Back link content is a gap.
HTML field name is 'telephone' but the label is 'Mobile number' - confirm the intended field semantics (mobile vs any phone) for the rebuild.

The tbody showed a first (empty) contact row carrying only a Remove button plus a second row holding the three inputs - clarify the exact repeat-row template shape (how an added contact vs the entry row differ) from the page object/template.

QA coverage of this page is minimal and does not raise the requirements lower bound: the page object (NominatedContactsPage.ts:6-8) exposes only btnSaveAndContinue, and every CHED-D workflow/accessibility path clicks straight through with all fields blank (ched-d-workflows.ts:339-340, :659-660; ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:165-167). No test ever fills a contact, adds/removes a row, or asserts a validation message. So the Add another person / Remove behaviour, the 5-contact cap, partial-row acceptance and all field-format rules are exercised ONLY by the single happy-path trace (skipped empty) and remain unverified by tests.
